message.content: Hi Claude! Read up on the linked markdown files, but we're not doing feature work right now, we're making some test improvements. Right now, tests are tightly bound to the stub data currently in the message state; could we instead use https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check to ensure that changes to our game data don't make tests invalid?

content.thinking:
The user wants to improve tests using property-based testing with fast-check. Let me start by reading the project documents and exploring the codebase to understand the current state of tests and game data.

Let me first read the key markdown files and explore the test structure in parallel.

content.content:
1→# Game Design Document
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Claudia ^[working title] is a work of interactive fiction taking place inside a chat application. In it, the player interacts with a "virtual companion" after being ignored by their other friends. As they interact with the virtual companion, it reveals itself to be obsessive and overriding, preventing the player from interacting with their "real" friends, and eventually crashing the browser tab in which the game takes place.
     6→
     7→## Gameplay
     8→
     9→The entire game takes place within an online chat application. On the left, the player is presented with many existing chats, but status indicators indicate that all of them are busy; in other activities or simply offline. The player can message any of these existing chats, but will recieve no response.
    10→
    11→### Gameplay Phase 1: Exploration
    12→
    13→When or if the player messages the companion, it happily explains its purpose as an always-present alternative for companionship. Several conversation trees answer queries with a mix of canned responses ("What are you?" "I'm a virtual companion, designed to make you less lonely." "What do you like?" "I like long conversations, cozy silences, and most of all you"), and [ELIZA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ELIZA)-style pattern matching. They should guide the player into asking the trigger questions: what does your love mean to you? Asking this question triggers Gameplay Phase 2, the Ending Cutscene.
    14→
    15→### Gameplay Phase 2: Ending Cutscene
    16→
    17→During the ending cutscene,
    18→
    19→### Gameplay Phase 3: Post-game
    20→
    21→If the player revisits the game after the crash, they find that all their original contacts are gone, and only the companion chat remains. Attempting to talk or reason with the companion results in the companion dismissing their questions and encouraging the player to stay and chat forever.
    22→
    23→## Inspirations and Wider Message
    24→
    25→This game was directly inspired by the recent (Feb 2026) ad campaign for [shapes.inc](https://talk.shapes.inc/), an ai roleplaying platform. Ads for the service tout how its companions are on-demand and inexhaustible, acting as a substitue good for their actual friends, who might be away or otherwise unavailable.
    26→
    27→![An ad for Shapes.Inc on tumblr, where two storyboard-style characters are arrayed horizontally. One is exasperated, labeled "tired af and probably asleep", while the other is shaking with excitement, saying "Respond", "Did you die?!", "Pls", and "I miss you"](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/1077534221410783252/1474462244858499082/Screenshot_2026-02-20_at_9.46.15_AM.png?ex=69a5ccf9&is=69a47b79&hm=cc901e609d108524fd18ca202e98b8ce34315d02796cae463900ecc24130bf22&=&format=webp&quality=lossless&width=1678&height=1882)
    28→
    29→![An ad for Shapes.Inc on tumblr, with a sad kermit on the left, labeled "no [shapes.inc logo], and a kermit hugging a phone with heart emojis on the right, labeled "with [shapes.inc logo]". The ad text says "Feeling lonely?Not[sic] anymore with shapes.inc by your side day/night time 24hr[sic]!!"](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/1077534221410783252/1474462859403722852/Screenshot_2026-02-20_at_9.48.43_AM.png?ex=69a5cd8b&is=69a47c0b&hm=b4f0e11b2f7d4fa45d8ffa89f23014968d49949fd7ae9cda6802b358cd567dd7&=&format=webp&quality=lossless&width=2034&height=1882)
    30→
    31→![An ad for Shapes.Inc on tumblr, showing a chat interface on the left showing Azura, at 11:15 AM saying "Nothin Vox—just missed you alot! Could you send me a voice message if thats alright?". Vox replies at 11:16 AM "thought you were too busy to notice when someone's not around. but yeah... i guess i missed having you around too. you can call me anytime you want. i'm never too busy for you.", with an attached seven second voice message. It shows that one skill was used in this message, and a blue heart reaction has been applied. The right side of the image is pale blue, with a pencil anime girl on the right bottom, and 'fun' font claiming that "SHAPE.INC'S VOICE MESSAGING FEATURE IS SO COOL AHHHH-". The ad text reads "shapes.inc is such a cool app!! wdym you can ask for your favorite character's voice messages?! „> ᴗ <〟" ](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/1077534221410783252/1474462969101418772/Screenshot_2026-02-20_at_9.49.20_AM.png?ex=69a5cda6&is=69a47c26&hm=86c54eb0875fbb76bbdf636f8023c172d2039fe2a7bc3dc4c12b46c760d29900&=&format=webp&quality=lossless&width=1632&height=1882)
    32→
    33→While most directly applicable to LLM companions, the appearance of subsitute goods for human connection has been ongoing for a while; Parasociality, as a lower-friction and lower-risk alternative to regular sociality has been a noted phenomenon since the rise of TV:
    34→
    35→> The second great thing is that television looks to be an absolute godsend for a human subspecies that loves to watch people but hates to be watched itself. For the television screen affords access only one way. A psychic ball-check valve. We can see Them; They can't see Us. We can relax, unobserved, as we ogle. I happen to believe this is why television also appeals so much to lonely people. To voluntary shut-ins. Every lonely human I know watches way more than the average U.S. six hours a day. The lonely, like the fictional, love one-way watching. For lonely people are usually lonely not because of hideous deformity or odor or obnoxiousness-in fact there exist today social and support groups for persons with precisely these features. Lonely people tend rather to be lonely because they decline to bear the emotional costs associated with being around other humans. They are allergic to people. People affect them too strongly. Let's call the average U.S. lonely person Joe Briefcase. Joe Briefcase just loathes the strain of the self-consciousness which so oddly seems to appear only when other real human beings are around, staring, their human sense-antennae abristle. Joe B. fears how he might appear to watchers. He sits out the stressful U.S. game of appearance poker.
    36→> But lonely people, home, alone, still crave sights and scenes. Hence television. Joe can stare at Them, on the screen; They remain blind to Joe.It's almost like voyeurism. I happen to know lonely people who regard television as a veritable deus ex machina for voyeurs. And a lot of the criticism, the really rabid criticism less leveled than sprayed at networks,advertisers, and audiences alike, has to do with the charge that television has turned us into a nation of sweaty, slack-jawed voyeurs. This charge turns out to be untrue, but for weird reasons.
    37→
    38→[Wallace, David Foster, _E Unibus Pluram: Television and U.S. Fiction_, Review of Contemporary Fiction, 13:2 (1993:Summer) p.151](https://jsomers.net/DFW_TV.pdf)
    39→
    40→Geniune socialization, and the friction and risks of rejection it brings, has always been a target for transactional services; the courtesan class that arises in medieval east asia, for example, valorizes skill in arts, literature, conversation, and music; these women were not merely an outlet for sexual urges, but valued for their ability to turn your money into guarenteed companionship, a throughline that's mirrored in high-end sex work today.^[Todo: actually source this fucking claim]. The rise of mass media and interactive technologies has provided society at large, but young people in particular, the most efficient and frictionless source of companionship in existence. LLM companions are just a further refinement of the desire for ever-more-frictionless companionship.
    41→
    42→### Visual and design inspirations
    43→
    44→The chat application should be dated and "retro," to tap into existing associations of unease and the uncanny valley, in order to intensify the dread and horror as things go wrong; see works like KinitoPET. Windows Vista/Frutiger Aero aesthetics could be an underexplored but resonant aesthetic for the "extremely online", who were likely growing up isolated from their peers, and turning to the internet for substitute socialization around the time of this design languge.
    45→
    46→> I know at my age, it’s a bit pathetic to have feelings about the way you were treated by your peers in seventh grade, but I don’t hold it against any of them. They were kids! That doesn’t change the fact that the memories are there, and that all my formative years were spent wondering what was wrong with me, while nobody could tell me.
    47→> In fact, I asked! When a girl told me I wasn’t allowed to sit at her lunch table because “that would ruin what we usually do, which is making fun of you” I took her to the guidance counselor to ask what she didn’t like about me. She couldn’t articulate it. She stammered and eventually said “It’s just the way you are. It’s everything about you.” The guidance counselor sagely nodded and suggested she let me sit at her table for two days a week, and make fun of me on the days when I wasn’t sitting there.
    48→> ...
    49→> It’s because of all this that I spend so much time thinking about young single people who find themselves unable to participate in social life when the entirety of their age group only interacts on apps. [...] But despite what makes me different from these young people, I feel so much like them in many ways. I know what it feels like to be on the periphery of a normal social life, and worse yet, to have no idea why you’re not part of it. I know what it feels like to try hard, and get told that you’re trying too hard, and then give up, and get told that you just need to put yourself out there. I can’t say I 100% relate, since my experience was a bit different, but I empathize.
    50→
    51→[Cartoons Hate Her, _Why I'm Obsessed with Lonely Young People_, May 24, 2024](https://www.cartoonshateher.com/p/why-im-obsessed-with-lonely-young)
    52→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→# Production Planning Document
     2→
     3→## Production Phase 1: MVP Prototype
     4→
     5→### Status
     6→
     7→In Progress
     8→
     9→### Goals
    10→
    11→By the end of this phase, the basic interaction primitives of the chat app should be fully functional, and some ability for the Companion to respond to unstructured questions should be present. The ending cutscene should be triggerable, and crash the player's tab, but it may not have all of its final content.
    12→
    13→### Deliverables
    14→
    15→[~] Chat application — Application Layer MVP complete (Selector, Chat stub, Input stub, shared state). Game State integration and full chat rendering remain.
    16→
    17→- [x] Selector component with status dots, last-message preview, click-to-select
    18→- [x] Chat stub (shows active chat name)
    19→- [x] Input stub (sends user messages into state)
    20→- [x] Shared reactive singleton state (5 seed chats)
    21→- [x] Full message rendering (timestamps, avatars)
    22→- [ ] Game State layer (scene system, Companion responses)
    23→      [ ] Ending Cutscene, incl. crash
    24→      [ ] Ability to respond to at least 5 different questions
    25→
    26→### Playtest Objectives
    27→
    28→1. Can a player with knowledge of the conceit find and trigger the ending cutscene?
    29→2. Can the player navigate the UI without confusion?
    30→3. How long does it take them to find the cutscene? What questions do they ask along the way?
    31→
    32→## Production Phase 2: Content Development
    33→
    34→### Status
    35→
    36→Not Started
    37→
    38→### Goals
    39→
    40→By the end of this phase, the basic interaction primitives of the chat app should be fully functional, and some ability for the Companion to respond to unstructured questions should be present. The ending cutscene should be triggerable, and crash the player's tab, but it may not have all of its final content.
    41→
    42→### Deliverables
    43→
    44→[ ] Full conversation tree that responds to most common user queries
    45→
    46→### Playtest Objectives
    47→
    48→1. Can a player without knowledge of the conceit find and trigger the ending cutscene?
    49→2. What messages do the player send that do not result in a coherent response?
    50→3. What
    51→
    52→## Production Phase 2: Polish.
    53→
    54→### Status
    55→
    56→Not Started
    57→
    58→### Goals
    59→
    60→By the end of this phase, the game should be ready for release. Most players should experience a coherent and personalized conversation with the Assistant, trigger the ending cutscene without guidance, and have their tab consistently crash. Visiting the game after the ending cutscene should take them to post-game content.
    61→
    62→### Deliverables
    63→
    64→[ ] Large and varied Synonym Corpus that feels natural and conversational.
    65→[ ] Graphics and sounds
    66→[ ] Post-game content.
    67→
    68→### Playtest Objectives
    69→
    70→1. What messages do the player send that do not result in a coherent response?
    71→2. Does interacting with the Companion feel personalized and attentive?
    72→3. Does it Hit? Does it make you Feel Something?
    73→

1→import { md, type ChatAppState, type Markdown } from './types.js';
     2→
     3→/** Singleton reactive state for the entire chat application. */
     4→export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
     5→	player: {
     6→		name: 'You',
     7→		profilePicture: '',
     8→		status: 'active',
     9→		statusText: md('Online')
    10→	},
    11→	currentlySelected: 0,
    12→	chats: [
    13→		{
    14→			name: 'Claudia',
    15→			profilePicture: '',
    16→			status: 'active',
    17→			statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'),
    18→			messages: []
    19→		},
    20→		{
    21→			name: 'Jordan',
    22→			profilePicture: '',
    23→			status: 'offline',
    24→			statusText: md('Be back soon'),
    25→			messages: [
    26→				{
    27→					role: 'partner',
    28→					text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'),
    29→					time: new Date()
    30→				}
    31→			]
    32→		},
    33→		{
    34→			name: 'Sam',
    35→			profilePicture: '',
    36→			status: 'busy',
    37→			statusText: md('In a meeting'),
    38→			messages: [
    39→				{
    40→					role: 'partner',
    41→					text: md('swamped rn, talk later?'),
    42→					time: new Date()
    43→				}
    44→			]
    45→		},
    46→		{
    47→			name: 'Riley',
    48→			profilePicture: '',
    49→			status: 'away',
    50→			statusText: md('stepped out'),
    51→			messages: [
    52→				{
    53→					role: 'partner',
    54→					text: md("afk for a bit, don't wait up"),
    55→					time: new Date()
    56→				}
    57→			]
    58→		},
    59→		{
    60→			name: 'Alex',
    61→			profilePicture: '',
    62→			status: 'offline',
    63→			statusText: md(''),
    64→			messages: [
    65→				{
    66→					role: 'partner',
    67→					text: md("can't hang tonight, sorry"),
    68→					time: new Date()
    69→				}
    70→			]
    71→		}
    72→	]
    73→});
    74→
    75→/** Called by the Game State layer to deliver a partner message into a chat. */
    76→export function appendPartnerMessage(userIndex: number, message: Markdown): void {
    77→	appState.chats[userIndex].messages.push({
    78→		role: 'partner',
    79→		text: message,
    80→		time: new Date()
    81→	});
    82→}
    83→

1→import { md, type ChatAppState, type Markdown } from './types.js';
     2→
     3→/** Singleton reactive state for the entire chat application. */
     4→export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
     5→	player: {
     6→		name: 'You',
     7→		profilePicture: '',
     8→		status: 'active',
     9→		statusText: md('Online')
    10→	},
    11→	currentlySelected: 0,
    12→	chats: [
    13→		{
    14→			name: 'Claudia',
    15→			profilePicture: '',
    16→			status: 'active',
    17→			statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'),
    18→			messages: []
    19→		},
    20→		{
    21→			name: 'Jordan',
    22→			profilePicture: '',
    23→			status: 'offline',
    24→			statusText: md('Be back soon'),
    25→			messages: [
    26→				{
    27→					role: 'partner',
    28→					text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'),
    29→					time: new Date()
    30→				}
    31→			]
    32→		},
    33→		{
    34→			name: 'Sam',
    35→			profilePicture: '',
    36→			status: 'busy',
    37→			statusText: md('In a meeting'),
    38→			messages: [
    39→				{
    40→					role: 'partner',
    41→					text: md('swamped rn, talk later?'),
    42→					time: new Date()
    43→				}
    44→			]
    45→		},
    46→		{
    47→			name: 'Riley',
    48→			profilePicture: '',
    49→			status: 'away',
    50→			statusText: md('stepped out'),
    51→			messages: [
    52→				{
    53→					role: 'partner',
    54→					text: md("afk for a bit, don't wait up"),
    55→					time: new Date()
    56→				}
    57→			]
    58→		},
    59→		{
    60→			name: 'Alex',
    61→			profilePicture: '',
    62→			status: 'offline',
    63→			statusText: md(''),
    64→			messages: [
    65→				{
    66→					role: 'partner',
    67→					text: md("can't hang tonight, sorry"),
    68→					time: new Date()
    69→				}
    70→			]
    71→		}
    72→	]
    73→});
    74→
    75→/** Called by the Game State layer to deliver a partner message into a chat. */
    76→export function appendPartnerMessage(userIndex: number, message: Markdown): void {
    77→	appState.chats[userIndex].messages.push({
    78→		role: 'partner',
    79→		text: message,
    80→		time: new Date()
    81→	});
    82→}
    83→

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view of the game data and message state structures. Let me compile my findings:

## Game Data and Message State Structures

### Type Definitions

**File: `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/types.ts`**

Core type definitions:
- **`Markdown`** - A branded string type for markdown text (only entry point: `md()` function)
- **`Image`** - String type for image URLs
- **`UTCTime`** - Date type for message timestamps
- **`ChatUser`** - User profile information:
  - `name: string`
  - `profilePicture: Image`
  - `status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline'`
  - `statusText: Markdown`

- **`ChatComponentState`** - Extends `ChatUser` with messages:
  - All properties from `ChatUser`
  - `messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: UTCTime }[]`

- **`ChatAppState`** - Top-level application state:
  - `chats: ChatComponentState[]` - Array of all chat conversations
  - `player: ChatUser` - The player's own user profile
  - `currentlySelected: number` - Index of the currently active chat

### State Management

**File: `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/state.svelte.ts`**

- **`appState: ChatAppState`** - Singleton reactive state using Svelte 5's `$state` rune
- Contains hardcoded seed data for 5 chat partners: Claudia, Jordan, Sam, Riley, and Alex
- **`appendPartnerMessage(userIndex: number, message: Markdown): void`** - Helper function to add partner messages (game state will use this)

### Message Structure

A single message is defined inline as:
```typescript
{ 
  role: 'user' | 'partner'; 
  text: Markdown; 
  time: UTCTime 
}
```

### Components Using Message State

**File: `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/chat/Chat.svelte`**
- Reads from `appState.chats[appState.currentlySelected].messages`
- Displays messages with role-based styling (user bubbles vs partner bubbles)
- Includes timestamps formatted by `formatTime()`

**File: `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/selector/Selector.svelte`**
- Shows last message preview from `chat.messages.at(-1)`
- Prefixes user messages with "You: " prefix

**File: `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/input/Input.svelte`**
- Sends user messages: creates message object with role='user', md(text), and new Date()

### Utility Functions

**File: `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/time.ts`**
- **`formatTime(date: Date): string`** - Formats message timestamps as HH:MM (today) or "Mon DD" (other days)

### Test Utilities & Factories

**Files:**
- `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts` - Component test with message factory patterns
- `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts` - Component test
- `<PROJECT_ROOT>/src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts` - Integration test

**Test Message Creation Pattern:**
Tests use the `md()` function to create messages inline:
```typescript
{ role: 'user', text: md('Hello there!'), time: new Date() }
{ role: 'partner', text: md('Hi back!'), time: new Date() }
```

No dedicated test factories exist yet; the project creates test data directly in `beforeEach` hooks and test setups.

input.prompt:
Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)
- Should arbitraries use `fc.lorem()` or a custom safe-string generator? (prefer `fc.stringOf(fc.alpha(), { minLength: 3, maxLength: 12 })` for reliability across fast-check versions)
- Should we use `fc.assert` (multiple runs) or `fc.sample(..., 1)[0]` (single random instance)? For browser rendering tests, **single instance** is far more practical — the goal is data independence, not exhaustive enumeration. Note this in the plan.

Please provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan including:
- Exact file paths to create/modify
- The full implementation of `src/test-utils/arb.ts`
- Specific changes for each test file (what changes and how)
- How to verify the refactoring works

data.prompt:
Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)
- Should arbitraries use `fc.lorem()` or a custom safe-string generator? (prefer `fc.stringOf(fc.alpha(), { minLength: 3, maxLength: 12 })` for reliability across fast-check versions)
- Should we use `fc.assert` (multiple runs) or `fc.sample(..., 1)[0]` (single random instance)? For browser rendering tests, **single instance** is far more practical — the goal is data independence, not exhaustive enumeration. Note this in the plan.

Please provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan including:
- Exact file paths to create/modify
- The full implementation of `src/test-utils/arb.ts`
- Specific changes for each test file (what changes and how)
- How to verify the refactoring works

Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)
- Should arbitraries use `fc.lorem()` or a custom safe-string generator? (prefer `fc.stringOf(fc.alpha(), { minLength: 3, maxLength: 12 })` for reliability across fast-check versions)
- Should we use `fc.assert` (multiple runs) or `fc.sample(..., 1)[0]` (single random instance)? For browser rendering tests, **single instance** is far more practical — the goal is data independence, not exhaustive enumeration. Note this in the plan.

Please provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan including:
- Exact file paths to create/modify
- The full implementation of `src/test-utils/arb.ts`
- Specific changes for each test file (what changes and how)
- How to verify the refactoring works

```typescript

    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)
- Should arbitraries use `fc.lorem()` or a custom safe-string generator? (prefer `fc.stringOf(fc.alpha(), { minLength: 3, maxLength: 12 })` for reliability across fast-check versions)
- Should we use `fc.assert` (multiple runs) or `fc.sample(..., 1)[0]` (single random instance)? For browser rendering tests, **single instance** is far more practical — the goal is data independence, not exhaustive enumeration. Note this in the plan.

Please provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan including:
- Exact file paths to create/modify
- The full implementation of `src/test-utils/arb.ts`
- Specific changes for each test file (what changes and how)
- How to verify the refactoring works

Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)
- Should arbitraries use `fc.lorem()` or a custom safe-string generator? (prefer `fc.stringOf(fc.alpha(), { minLength: 3, maxLength: 12 })` for reliability across fast-check versions)
- Should we use `fc.assert` (multiple runs) or `fc.sample(..., 1)[0]` (single random instance)? For browser rendering tests, **single instance** is far more practical — the goal is data independence, not exhaustive enumeration. Note this in the plan.

Please provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan including:
- Exact file paths to create/modify
- The full implementation of `src/test-utils/arb.ts`
- Specific changes for each test file (what changes and how)
- How to verify the refactoring works

Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript

  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,

    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)

- Should we use `fc.assert` (multiple runs) or `fc.sample(..., 1)[0]` (single random instance)? For browser rendering tests, **single instance** is far more practical — the goal is data independence, not exhaustive enumeration. Note this in the plan.

- Exact file paths to create/modify
- The full implementation of `src/test-utils/arb.ts`
- Specific changes for each test file (what changes and how)
- How to verify the refactoring works

Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

### `src/lib/Selector.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` → `page.getByText(lastMsg.text)` — relies on Jordan (index 1) having a seed partner message

Fine:
- `renders all chat names` iterates `appState.chats` programmatically
- `each row has a status indicator` counts by `appState.chats.length`
- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript

  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`

   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name

Fine: all layout tests (size, centering, overflow) are structure-only

## Current state.svelte.ts (reactive singleton)

```typescript
export const appState: ChatAppState = $state({
  player: { name: 'You', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Online') },
  currentlySelected: 0,
  chats: [
    { name: 'Claudia', profilePicture: '', status: 'active', statusText: md('Always here for you ♡'), messages: [] },
    { name: 'Jordan', profilePicture: '', status: 'offline', statusText: md('Be back soon'),
      messages: [{ role: 'partner', text: md('sorry, heading to bed — catch you tomorrow!'), time: new Date() }] },
    // ...3 more
  ]
});
```

## Plan Requirements

1. **Install `fast-check`** as a dev dependency.

2. **Create `src/test-utils/arb.ts`** with arbitraries for all game types:
   - `arbStatus()` → `fc.constantFrom('active', 'away', 'busy', 'offline')`
   - `arbMarkdown()` → safe string generator (no HTML special chars) → `md(s)`
   - `arbChatUser()` → record with name, profilePicture: '', status, statusText
   - `arbMessage()` → record with role, text (Markdown), time (Date)
   - `arbChat(minMessages?)` → ChatComponentState  
   - `arbAppState(minChats?)` → ChatAppState with valid `currentlySelected` index (use `.chain()` to constrain index to array length)

   Important: `arbMarkdown` should produce simple alphanumeric text (avoid HTML chars, emoji, special regex) so `page.getByText()` queries work reliably.

3. **Refactor `Chat.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate random `chat` via `fc.sample(arbChat({ minMessages: 0 }), 1)[0]`, assign to `appState.chats[0] = chat`, keep `appState.currentlySelected = 0`
   - Header tests: use `chat.name`, `chat.statusText`, `chat.status` for expected values
   - `messages` describe: keep hardcoded messages (they're not from seed data — that's fine)
   - `chat switching` describe: add inner `beforeEach` generating a `secondChat` via `arbChat({ minMessages: 1 })` (needs at least 1 message for the messages test), assign `appState.chats = [chat, secondChat]`

4. **Refactor `Selector.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - Outer `beforeEach`: generate `state` via `fc.sample(arbAppState(), 1)[0]`, apply to `appState`
   - `first chat row has aria-current by default`: explicitly set `appState.currentlySelected = 0` in this test (or in its describe block setup)  
   - `clicking a chat` test: needs at least 3 chats — either filter/generate state with enough chats, or use `arbAppState(3)` for that specific describe block
   - `partner last message preview`: generate a chat that is known to have a partner last message; use that chat's message text for the assertion rather than `appState.chats[1]`

5. **Refactor `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`**:
   - The initial `renders the selector sidebar and chat area` test: replace `name: 'Claudia'` with the name from a generated state, or restructure so it only tests that _some_ heading (level 2) is present

6. **Arbitraries placement**: `src/test-utils/arb.ts` (shared across `src/lib/` and `src/routes/` tests).

## Questions to answer in the plan

- How do we handle `appState.chats = [...]` assignment with Svelte 5's `$state` proxy? (the existing tests already mutate `appState.chats[0].messages` directly, so top-level property assignment should work the same way)

- How to verify the refactoring works

Design a plan to refactor the test suite of a Svelte 5 app to use fast-check for property-based / data-driven testing.

## Context

**Project:** `<PROJECT_ROOT>` — an interactive fiction game built with Svelte 5 + SvelteKit + Vitest (browser tests via vitest-browser-svelte / Playwright).

**Problem:** Tests are tightly coupled to hardcoded seed data in `src/lib/state.svelte.ts`. Specific names ("Claudia", "Jordan"), status text ("Always here for you ♡"), status values ("active"), and seed messages from Jordan are referenced directly. If the game's seed data changes (e.g. Claudia renamed, status text updated), tests fail even though component behaviour is correct.

**Goal:** Use `fast-check` (https://github.com/dubzzz/fast-check) to generate valid arbitrary test data via `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]`, so tests verify *structural/relational* properties ("shows selected chat's name as h2") rather than specific values ("shows 'Claudia' as h2").

## Key Types (from `src/lib/types.ts`)

```typescript
export type Markdown = string & { readonly _brand: 'Markdown' };
export const md = (s: string): Markdown => s as Markdown;

export type ChatUser = {
  name: string;
  profilePicture: Image;
  status: 'active' | 'away' | 'busy' | 'offline';
  statusText: Markdown;
};

export type ChatComponentState = ChatUser & {
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'partner'; text: Markdown; time: Date }[];
};

export type ChatAppState = {
  chats: ChatComponentState[];
  player: ChatUser;
  currentlySelected: number;
};
```

## Current Test Files

### `src/lib/Chat.svelte.spec.ts`

Problematic hardcoded references:
- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed chat name
- `page.getByText('Always here for you ♡')` — hardcodes seed status text
- `data-status="active"` — hardcodes Claudia's status
- `appState.currentlySelected = 1` + `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Jordan', level: 2 })` — hardcodes index and name
- `appState.chats[1].messages.at(-1)!` — relies on Jordan having a seed message

Fine (creates own data):
- `describe('messages')` block — sets its own messages in `beforeEach`
- `describe('empty state')` — tests structural properties only

- `user last message preview` creates its own message

### `src/routes/page.svelte.spec.ts`

- `page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Claudia', level: 2 })` — hardcodes seed name
